div1.entryFree:
Gnaw (impf. --ed, R3 I, 4, 25; partic. gnawn, Wiv. II, 2, 307) 1) to wear off by slow corrosion with the fore teeth: H6A III, 1, 73. H6B III, 1, 192. R3 II, 4, 28. IV, 2, 27 (Qq bites). Cor. I, 1, 254. Tit. III, 1, 262. Tim. IV, 3, 49. Oth. V, 2, 43. With an accus. denoting the result: --ing my bonds in sunder, Err. V, 249. Figuratively: the thought doth like a poisonous mineral g. my inwards, Oth. II, 1, 306. hell g. his bones, IV, 2, 136. Absol. the --ing vulture of thy mind, Tit. V, 2, 31.
2) intr.: my reputation gnawn at, Wiv. II, 2, 307. men that fishes --ed upon, R3 I, 4, 25.
